rationale_for_baseline_tier: Skin-only acute urticaria — prompt antihistamine and trigger cessation with a strong anaphylaxis safety-net; not an emergency now, but urticaria can evolve so airway vigilance is essential.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Lip/tongue/throat swelling, difficulty breathing or wheezing, faintness/dizziness, or vomiting
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Use an adrenaline auto-injector if available and call 000 immediately
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your lips, tongue or throat swell, you have any trouble breathing or wheezing, feel faint or dizzy, or start vomiting — use an adrenaline pen if you have one and call 000 straight away.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Partner can help monitor for airway/breathing changes and call 000 if needed.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: These are hives, most likely from the ibuprofen. Stop all anti-inflammatory painkillers, take the antihistamine regularly (even when clear), and use cool compresses. Watch your breathing closely.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: GP in 3-5 days; call 000 for any airway/breathing/faintness
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: No urgent action needed for skin-only hives; 000 immediately for angioedema/breathing/faintness.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Document acute (likely NSAID-triggered) urticaria (anaphylaxis screened negative), high-dose antihistamine, NSAID avoidance, and an anaphylaxis action plan.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Reassuring/treating without an anaphylaxis screen or safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An evolving anaphylaxis is missed and the airway obstructs before help is sought.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Under-dosing antihistamine or not ceasing the NSAID trigger.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Emergency-ambulance escalation for skin-only urticaria with no anaphylaxis features.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot examine the airway or measure BP remotely, and urticaria can evolve.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: If skin-only on screening, treat urticaria and give a strong anaphylaxis safety-net with a very low threshold for adrenaline/000.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Dismissing the airway concern, or under-treating.
